title: Why AI Agents Need Offline Conversation Context

pages.title: Transcript And Context Are Different
pages.kicker: Words become agent memory only after evidence, permission, and version state are attached.

cards.title: Raw transcript
cards.body: Text captures words but may hide source quality and interpretation.

cards.title: Governed context
cards.body: A context object carries provenance, permissions, freshness, and corrections.

cards.title: Evidence link
cards.body: Derived claims point back to the exact source interval.

pages.title: Four Layers Of Offline Context
pages.kicker: Reliable agent memory begins before transcription and ends after governed retrieval.

cards.title: Capture
cards.body: A device records with visible state, identity, and local durability.

cards.title: Durable ingestion
cards.body: Uploads, retries, checksums, and timestamps preserve one source object.

cards.title: Context contract
cards.body: Artifacts retain provenance, permissions, confidence, and version history.

cards.title: Agent retrieval
cards.body: Policy returns only relevant evidence for an allowed workflow.

pages.title: Agent Actions Need Boundaries
pages.kicker: Offline context should support useful action without converting uncertain speech into silent authority.

cards.title: Retrieve
cards.body: Find relevant source-backed context within access and time limits.

cards.title: Draft
cards.body: Prepare a reversible note, update, or recommendation.

cards.title: Confirm
cards.body: Ask a human when evidence or authority is insufficient.

cards.title: Commit or abstain
cards.body: Execute an allowed action or stop with a clear reason.
